executive_summary: The ANAO is a small, specialist integrity agency that audits the Commonwealth on behalf of the Parliament. Its 2025-26 plan is dominated by enabling and corporate themes rather than program delivery: a flagged budget shortfall (performance audit targets cut from a JCPAA-recommended 48 to 38-42), a new digital strategy and AI policy, persistent workforce attrition and talent competition, and a drive to integrate and streamline its audit products. Because ANAO is itself the supreme audit institution and prizes independence, it is a cautious and conflict-sensitive buyer of external advice, and any work touching core audit functions is unlikely. Credible, proportionate opportunities sit in its corporate enablers — workforce/capability, digital and AI investment governance, and financial sustainability/costing — where independent advice does not compromise its mandate. Expect modest engagement sizes (short advisory or framework pieces), not multi-year delivery.

strategic_priorities.title: Impact where it matters
strategic_priorities.description: Maximise the value and use of audit work by the Parliament, support the establishment of the 48th Parliament and the JCPAA, deepen public sector engagement, and trial integrated financial/performance statements audits and a 'rapid review' performance audit approach to reduce duplication and improve timeliness.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: we will trial audit approaches for financial statements and performance statements audits that can be integrated to reduce duplication ... develop a 'rapid review' approach to performance auditing

strategic_priorities.title: Performance through innovation
strategic_priorities.description: Embed innovation in audit products and processes, adopt approved AI tools and an automation program to lift productivity and quality, apply a sharper risk focus to audit procedures, and develop a digital strategy to become a data-enabled organisation.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: we will use approved artificial intelligence (AI) tools and other technology within audit work to improve productivity while maintaining quality

strategic_priorities.title: Inspire and develop our people
strategic_priorities.description: Build a skilled, adaptable, diverse workforce able to work flexibly across audit products; grow specialist capability (including climate-change disclosure auditing and AI use) via the ANAO Academy; and manage high attrition through workforce planning, development and retention.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Attrition is typically high in the profession. To best manage attrition, we focus on investing in high-quality training and creating a great place to work.

reform_and_change_drivers: Budget constraint: current funding does not support the JCPAA-recommended 48 performance audits, with targets reduced to 38-42 reports to protect mandatory functions
Establishment of the 48th Parliament and re-establishment of the JCPAA, which reviews ANAO reports and budget
New audit product: ramping up performance statements audits and developing in-house capability for climate-change disclosure auditing
Adoption of AI and automation in audit work, requiring a new AI policy, safeguards and staff training
Development of a new ANAO digital strategy to become a 'data-enabled organisation'
Refresh of enterprise risks and a new Risk Management Framework 2025-27
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework intent to rebuild APS capability and limit reliance on contractors for core work
challenges_and_risks: Budget position constraining delivery of the audit work program (a named enterprise risk)
Workforce attraction and retention in a globally competitive audit talent market, with high sector attrition
Security of the sensitive information the ANAO holds, and cyber resilience
Capacity to deliver new/emerging audit areas (climate disclosures, AI-enabled auditing)
Investing sufficiently in tools and technology to audit contemporarily while maintaining quality
Maintaining institutional independence and reputation (risk appetite stated as 'low')

opportunity_areas.agency_need: High attrition, strong external competition for audit talent, and a need to grow capability across audit products and new fields (climate disclosures, AI).
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A short workforce planning and retention strategy review: model attrition and skills supply/demand across audit products, assess the ANAO Academy's 'grow our own' pipeline, and recommend retention levers and a resourcing strategy for emerging capability needs.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan repeatedly names attrition, competition for talent and workforce/capability risk as a focus, and elevates workforce planning as a 2025-26 activity — a clean, non-independence-sensitive fit.

opportunity_areas.area: Digital strategy and AI investment governance
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Developing a digital strategy and a new AI policy, running an automation program, and needing assurance that technology investment delivers value while managing risk.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Independent advice on the digital strategy and a business case / investment governance framework for the AI and automation program — including data governance and benefits tracking — explicitly advisory, not systems build.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to a new digital strategy, AI policy and automation program in 2025-26 and flags tooling investment as an enterprise risk; independent investment governance advice is credible and proportionate.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Funding does not cover the recommended audit volume, and the agency must demonstrate cost-per-audit efficiency (CPI-capped) across three programs while making the case for adequate resourcing.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A financial sustainability / costing review of the audit programs: an activity-based view of cost per audit product to support resource allocation, efficiency decisions and evidence for the JCPAA budget submission.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly states current funding cannot deliver 48 audits and uses CPI-capped cost-per-audit efficiency measures; an independent costing lens supports budget advocacy. Tempered because ANAO has strong in-house financial expertise.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Trialling integration of financial and performance statements audits and a 'rapid review' performance audit approach to cut duplication and improve timeliness.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A short operating-model / process design piece supporting the integration trial — mapping current vs future product workflows, hand-offs and resourcing implications — with change management support.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names a concrete intent to integrate audit products and develop rapid review approaches; however ANAO owns deep methodology expertise, so external help is at best a supporting role.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the ANAO Chief Operating Officer / corporate enabling group with a tailored workforce planning and retention proposition, referencing the plan's attrition and capability-risk language.
recommended_actions.rationale: Workforce is the most credible, independence-safe opportunity and is named as a 2025-26 enterprise risk and activity; a short, well-targeted advisory is proportionate to ANAO's size.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a one-page capability statement on independent digital/AI investment governance and data governance advice (explicitly not systems integration), timed to the development of the new digital strategy and AI policy.
recommended_actions.rationale: The digital strategy and AI policy are being built this year; positioning early as an independent adviser on governance and benefits (not delivery) fits both the need and the Consulting Firm's edge.

recommended_actions.action: Carefully assess and pre-empt independence/conflict perception before any approach, and frame all offerings as corporate/enabling support that does not touch audit methodology or opinions.
recommended_actions.rationale: ANAO is the sector's assurance body with a 'low' reputation risk appetite; mishandling the independence optics would foreclose any relationship. the Consulting Firm must lead with conflict-management discipline.

recommended_actions.action: Watch the JCPAA budget process and any tabled ANAO budget/resourcing recommendations for signals of funded uplift (digital, workforce) that could create procurement.
recommended_actions.rationale: The plan ties delivery capacity directly to the budget position determined via the JCPAA; funded outcomes there are where any external spend would originate.

engagement_targets: Chief Operating Officer / head of corporate and enabling services
People and capability branch / ANAO Academy lead (workforce planning, training)
Chief Information / Digital Officer or technology lead (digital strategy, AI, automation)
Chief Finance Officer (financial sustainability, audit costing)
Executive Board of Management (enterprise risk and strategic priorities owner)
talking_points: The plan names workforce attrition and talent competition as a standing risk — an independent, PGPA-fluent workforce and retention review can strengthen the 'grow our own' model without touching audit independence.

ANAO is building a digital strategy and AI policy this year; the Consulting Firm can provide independent investment-governance and data-governance advice (advisory, not systems build) to ensure the spend delivers measurable productivity and quality gains.
With funding unable to cover the JCPAA-recommended audit volume, an activity-based costing view of audit products could sharpen efficiency decisions and support the budget case to the Parliament.
We understand ANAO's independence and reputation sensitivities and would scope any engagement strictly to corporate/enabling functions, with explicit conflict-of-interest management.
The integration of financial and performance statements audits and the 'rapid review' approach are operating-model changes where independent process and change support can de-risk the transition.
caveats: This is a deliberately enabling-focused plan from the Commonwealth's own audit and assurance body. Several the Consulting Firm service lines (Assurance, Governance/Risk, Evaluation/Performance) are precisely ANAO's own core expertise, so pitching them would be non-credible and is avoided here. ANAO's strong independence stance, 'low' reputation risk appetite, alignment with the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework (favouring in-house work), and small budget all constrain external advisory demand. The genuine, proportionate opportunities are modest, corporate in nature (workforce, digital/AI investment governance, costing), and would require careful conflict-of-interest management given ANAO audits the sector the Consulting Firm serves. Confidences are set accordingly low/medium; no named procurement is evident in the plan.
